title: fix(python): raise IndexError in repeated field pop() for out-of-range index
author: jlaportebot
body:
## Summary

Fixes item 5 from #26596 — the upb C extension `RepeatedContainer_Pop()` was clamping out-of-range indices instead of raising `IndexError`, which is inconsistent with Python's built-in `list.pop()`.

## Bug Description

When calling `.pop()` with an out-of-range index on a protobuf repeated field using the upb (C) backend, the index was silently clamped to the last valid index instead of raising `IndexError`:

```python
msg.repeated_int32.extend(range(5))
msg.repeated_int32.pop(999999)  # Returns 4 instead of raising IndexError!
msg.repeated_int32.pop(-5)      # Also clamped instead of raising IndexError
```

Python's `list.pop()` raises `IndexError` in both cases. The pure Python fallback implementation already behaves correctly since `self._values[key]` on a list raises `IndexError` naturally.

## Changes

### `python/repeated.c`
- Removed the `#if UPB_FUTURE_REMOVE_POP_CLAMP` conditional clamping code
- Added proper bounds checking: raises `IndexError` with message `"pop index (%zd) out of range"` when index is out of range after negative-index normalization
- Matches `PyUpb_RepeatedContainer_Item()` which already raises `IndexError` for out-of-range access

### `python/google/protobuf/internal/message_test.py`
- Removed `@unittest.skipIf(api_implementation.Type() == 'upb', ...)` decorator from `testRepeatedPopOutOfBounds` since the upb implementation now correctly raises `IndexError`

## Test Plan

The existing `testRepeatedPopOutOfBounds` test (previously skipped for upb) now passes with this fix:
- `pop(-5)` on a 3-element list → `IndexError` ✓
- `pop(999999)` → `IndexError` ✓
- Normal `pop()`, `pop(0)`, `pop(1)`, `pop(-1)` still work correctly ✓

comments.author: runzw
comments.body: The issue was already fixed by a future breaking [change](https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/pull/26920) which will not be released until 27Q1 (notice that it's guarded by the breaking change flag).

comments.author: jlaportebot
comments.body: Thanks @runzw for the context. Since this is already fixed by #26920 (guarded by the breaking change flag, targeting 27Q1), I'll close this PR to avoid duplication. Appreciate the review!
